title: 2026 Oscars: Can Ryan Coogler Make Oscar History and Win Best Director?
text: The piece examines whether Ryan Coogler could become the first Black director to win Best Director at the Oscars in nearly a century. Coogler's film "Sinners" is competing against "One Battle After Another" for the award. Stone contends that "Sinners is the film that should be winning Best Picture" and describes it as "the most successful, critically acclaimed and relevant film of the year." She emphasizes the historical significance, noting that if Coogler wins, he would break a 98-year pattern where previous Best Picture winners directed by Black filmmakers failed to secure directing honors. The author expresses concern about the preferential ballot system and how voter rankings might affect the outcome. She acknowledges her uncertainty about the result while maintaining that Coogler's win would be deserved, stating that "years from now, no one will understand how or why it lost" if he doesn't prevail.
